   504→  const handleReduceBump = async (oppId: string) => {
   505→    if (isBumping) return;
   506→    setIsBumping(true);
   507→    // Optimistic update
   508→    setOpportunities(prev => prev.map(o =>
   509→      o.id === oppId ? { ...o, bump_number: Math.max(o.bump_number - 1, 0) } : o
   510→    ));
   511→    try {
   512→      await SalesPipelineService.reduceBump(oppId);
   513→      await fetchData();
   514→    } catch (err) {
   515→      console.error('Error reducing bump:', err);
   516→      await fetchData(); // revert on error
   517→    } finally {
   518→      setIsBumping(false);
   519→    }
   520→  };
   521→
   522→  const handleAddActivity = async () => {
   523→    if (!activityForm.title.trim() || !slideOverOpp) return;
   524→    setIsActivitySubmitting(true);
   525→    try {
   526→      await SalesPipelineService.createActivity({
   527→        ...activityForm,
   528→        opportunity_id: slideOverOpp.id,
   529→      });
   530→      setActivityForm({ opportunity_id: '', type: 'note', title: '' });
   531→      await fetchActivities(slideOverOpp.id);
   532→    } catch (err) {
   533→      console.error('Error adding activity:', err);
   534→    } finally {
   535→      setIsActivitySubmitting(false);
   536→    }
   537→  };
   538→
   539→  const handleInlineEdit = async (oppId: string, field: string, value: string) => {
   540→    try {
   541→      const updates: any = {};
   542→      if (field === 'deal_value') {
   543→        updates.deal_value = value ? parseFloat(value) : null;
   544→      } else if (field === 'temperature_score') {
   545→        updates.temperature_score = parseInt(value) || 50;
   546→      } else {
   547→        updates[field] = value || null;
   548→      }
   549→      await SalesPipelineService.update(oppId, updates);
   550→      await fetchData();
   551→    } catch (err) {
   552→      console.error('Error inline edit:', err);
   553→    }
   554→    setEditingCell(null);
   555→  };
   556→
   557→  const handleRecalculateAll = async () => {
   558→    setIsRecalculating(true);
   559→    try {
   560→      await SalesPipelineService.recalcAllTemperatures();
   561→      await fetchData();
   562→      if (activeTab === 'outreach') await fetchOutreach();
   563→    } catch (err) {
   564→      console.error('Error recalculating temperatures:', err);
   565→    } finally {
   566→      setIsRecalculating(false);
   567→    }
   568→  };
   569→
   570→  // ============================================
   571→  // DnD Handlers
   572→  // ============================================
   573→
   574→  const handleDragStart = (event: DragStartEvent) => {
   575→    const id = event.active.id as string;
   576→    setActiveId(id);
   577→    setActiveOpportunity(opportunities.find(o => o.id === id) || null);
   578→  };
   579→
   580→  const handleDragEnd = async (event: DragEndEvent) => {
   581→    const { active, over } = event;
   582→    setActiveId(null);
   583→    setActiveOpportunity(null);
   584→
   585→    if (!over) return;
   586→
   587→    const oppId = active.id as string;
   588→    const overId = over.id as string;
   589→    const opp = opportunities.find(o => o.id === oppId);
   590→    if (!opp) return;
   591→
   592→    // Check if dropped on a stage column
   593→    const isStageColumn = ALL_V2_STAGES.includes(overId as SalesPipelineStage);
   594→
   595→    if (isStageColumn) {
   596→      const newStage = overId as SalesPipelineStage;
   597→      if (opp.stage !== newStage) {
   598→        await handleStageChange(oppId, newStage, opp.stage);
   599→      }
   600→    } else {
   601→      // Dropped on another card — reorder within same stage
   602→      const targetOpp = opportunities.find(o => o.id === overId);
   603→      if (targetOpp && targetOpp.stage === opp.stage) {
   604→        const stageOpps = getStageOpps(opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage);
   605→        const oldIndex = stageOpps.findIndex(o => o.id === oppId);
   606→        const newIndex = stageOpps.findIndex(o => o.id === overId);
   607→        if (oldIndex !== -1 && newIndex !== -1) {
   608→          const reordered = arrayMove(stageOpps, oldIndex, newIndex);
   609→          const positions = reordered.map((o, i) => ({ id: o.id, position: i }));
   610→          await SalesPipelineService.updatePositions(positions);
   611→          await fetchData();
   612→        }
   613→      } else if (targetOpp && targetOpp.stage !== opp.stage) {
   614→        // Dropped on card in different stage
   615→        await handleStageChange(oppId, targetOpp.stage as SalesPipelineStage, opp.stage);
   616→      }
   617→    }
   618→  };
   619→
   620→  // ============================================
   621→  // Open slide-over
   622→  // ============================================
   623→
   624→  const openSlideOver = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity) => {
   625→    setSlideOverOpp(opp);
   626→    setSlideOverMode('view');
   627→    fetchActivities(opp.id);
   628→    setActivityForm({ opportunity_id: opp.id, type: 'note', title: '' });
   629→  };
   630→
   631→  const populateForm = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity) => {
   632→    setForm({
   633→      name: opp.name,
   634→      dm_account: opp.dm_account,
   635→      bucket: opp.bucket || undefined,
   636→      temperature_score: opp.temperature_score,
   637→      source: opp.source || undefined,
   638→      tg_handle: opp.tg_handle || undefined,
   639→      owner_id: opp.owner_id || undefined,
   640→      referrer: opp.referrer || undefined,
   641→      affiliate_id: opp.affiliate_id || undefined,
   642→      deal_value: opp.deal_value || undefined,
   643→      currency: opp.currency,
   644→      notes: opp.notes || undefined,
   645→    });
   646→  };
   647→
   648→  const openEditDialog = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity) => {
   649→    populateForm(opp);
   650→    if (slideOverOpp?.id === opp.id) {
   651→      // Editing from slide-over — switch to edit mode inline
   652→      setEditingOpp(opp);
   653→      setSlideOverMode('edit');
   654→    } else {
   655→      // Editing from table/kanban — open dialog
   656→      setEditingOpp(opp);
   657→    }
   658→  };
   659→
   660→  const toggleKanbanCollapse = (stage: string) => {
   661→    setCollapsedKanbanStages(prev => {
   662→      const next = new Set(prev);
   663→      next.has(stage) ? next.delete(stage) : next.add(stage);
   664→      return next;
   665→    });
   666→  };
   667→
   668→  // ============================================
   669→  // BAMFAM check
   670→  // ============================================
   671→
   672→  const isBAMFAM = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity) => {
   673→    const postDiscovery: SalesPipelineStage[] = ['discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract'];
   674→    if (!postDiscovery.includes(opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage)) return false;
   675→    if (!opp.next_meeting_at) return true;
   676→    return new Date(opp.next_meeting_at) < new Date();
   677→  };
   678→
   679→  // ============================================
   680→  // Helpers
   681→  // ============================================
   682→
   683→  const getUserName = (userId: string | null) => {
   684→    if (!userId) return '—';
   685→    const u = users.find(u => u.id === userId);
   686→    return u?.name || u?.email || '—';
   687→  };
   688→
   689→  const activityIcon = (type: ActivityType) => {
   690→    switch (type) {
   691→      case 'call': return <Phone className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />;
   692→      case 'message': return <MessageSquare className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />;
   693→      case 'meeting': return <Calendar className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />;
   694→      case 'proposal': return <FileText className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />;
   695→      case 'note': return <StickyNote className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />;
   696→      case 'bump': return <Zap className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />;
   697→    }
   698→  };
   699→
```

  1527→            >
  1528→              <ChevronLeft className="h-4 w-4" />
  1529→              Previous
  1530→            </Button>
  1531→            <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
  1532→              {Array.from({ length: Math.min(5, outreachTotalPages) }, (_, i) => {
  1533→                let pageNum: number;
  1534→                if (outreachTotalPages <= 5) {
  1535→                  pageNum = i + 1;
  1536→                } else if (outreachPage <= 3) {
  1537→                  pageNum = i + 1;
  1538→                } else if (outreachPage >= outreachTotalPages - 2) {
  1539→                  pageNum = outreachTotalPages - 4 + i;
  1540→                } else {
  1541→                  pageNum = outreachPage - 2 + i;
  1542→                }
  1543→                return (
  1544→                  <Button
  1545→                    key={pageNum}
  1546→                    variant={outreachPage === pageNum ? 'default' : 'outline'}
  1547→                    size="sm"
  1548→                    onClick={() => { setOutreachPage(pageNum); setSelectedOutreach([]); }}
  1549→                    className={`w-8 h-8 p-0 ${outreachPage === pageNum ? 'hover:opacity-90' : ''}`}
  1550→                    style={outreachPage === pageNum ? { backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' } : {}}
  1551→                  >
  1552→                    {pageNum}
  1553→                  </Button>
  1554→                );
  1555→              })}
  1556→            </div>
  1557→            <Button
  1558→              variant="outline"
  1559→              size="sm"
  1560→              onClick={() => { setOutreachPage(p => Math.min(outreachTotalPages, p + 1)); setSelectedOutreach([]); }}
  1561→              disabled={outreachPage === outreachTotalPages}
  1562→              className="flex items-center gap-1"
  1563→            >
  1564→              Next
  1565→              <ChevronRight className="h-4 w-4" />
  1566→            </Button>

  1583→        return (

  1594→            >
  1595→              <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
  1596→                {isCollapsed ? <ChevronRight className="h-4 w-4 text-orange-700" /> : <ChevronDown className="h-4 w-4 text-orange-700" />}
  1597→                <h4 className="font-semibold text-orange-700">{group.label}</h4>
  1598→                <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-xs font-medium">{group.opps.length}</Badge>
  1599→              </div>

## Comprehensive Analysis of Sales Pipeline Page Architecture

I've thoroughly explored the `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` file. Here's a detailed breakdown of the entire architecture:

---

### 1. TAB STRUCTURE (Outreach, Pipeline, Orbit)

**Three main tabs** controlled by `activeTab` state:

- **Outreach Tab** (`outreach`): Shows cold DMs in a paginated table with bulk actions
- **Pipeline Tab** (`pipeline`): Shows active pipeline opportunities in either table or kanban view
- **Orbit Tab** (`orbit`): Shows opportunities grouped by orbit reason (no_response, needs_timing, low_priority, etc.)

---

### 2. OUTREACH TAB DETAILED STRUCTURE

**Rows are rendered with the following structure:**

```
TableRow
├── Checkbox (with row number display on hover)
├── Name (with Building2 icon)
├── Bump Progress (N/4 with visual dots)
├── TG Handle
├── Source (capitalize)
├── Owner (user name)
├── Created Date
├── Last Bump Date
│  └── Quick Bump Button (inline with tooltip)
└── More Menu (dropdown)
    ├── Record Bump
    ├── Move to Warm
    ├── Edit
    └── Delete
```

**Outreach-specific actions:**
- **Bump buttons**: Individual bump in "Last Bump" column or bulk bump for selected rows
- **Bulk actions toolbar** (appears when selections > 0):
  - Select All on Page / Deselect All
  - Bulk Bump
  - Bulk Move to Warm
  - Bulk Delete
- **Pagination**: 50 items per page with page navigation
- **Filters**: 
  - Owner subtabs ("My Outreach", "All Owners", individual user names)
  - Search (debounced 400ms)
  - DM Account filter (Closer/SDR)
  - Bucket filter (A/B/C)
  - Bump Range filter (none/1-2/3+)

---

### 3. STATE MANAGEMENT - All useState Hooks

**Data state:**
```typescript
const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
const [opportunities, setOpportunities] = useState<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]>([]);
const [affiliates, setAffiliates] = useState<CRMAffiliate[]>([]);

```

```

```

---

### 4. SLIDE-OVER PANEL ARCHITECTURE

**Fixed positioned 480px-wide panel** rendered via `createPortal` to `document.body`

**View Mode (default):**
- Header with opportunity name, stage badge, bucket badge, DM account badge, deal value
- Edit button (switches to edit mode)
- Close button
- **Details Card**: Temperature (bar + %), Owner, TG Handle, Source, Next Meeting, Referrer, Last Contacted, Affiliate
- **Notes Section**: If notes exist
- **Bump Counter** (cold_dm only): 
  - Visual dots (1-4)
  - Minus button (reduce bump)
  - Plus button (record bump)
  - Last bump date
- **Quick Actions**: Move to Orbit button, Delete button
- **Stage Move**: Dropdown to change stage
- **Activity Timeline Section**:
  - Add activity form (type, title, description, outcome, next step, next step date picker)
  - Activity list (timeline view with icons, timestamps)

**Edit Mode (inline in slide-over):**
- Form fields: Name, DM Account, Bucket, Temperature slider, Source, TG Handle, Owner, Referrer, Affiliate, Deal Value, Currency, Notes
- Save/Cancel buttons

**Activity Types and Icons:**
- call → Phone icon
- message → MessageSquare icon
- meeting → Calendar icon
- proposal → FileText icon
- note → StickyNote icon
- bump → Zap icon

---

### 5. DASHBOARD SECTION STRUCTURE

**Collapsible dashboard** showing:

**Outreach Metrics (6 cards):**
- DMs Sent (total)
- Cold DMs (current)
- Response Rate %
- Meetings Booked
- Qualified % (A+B)
- Proposals Sent

**Closing Metrics (5 cards):**
- Discovery Calls
- Close Rate %
- Avg Deal Size
- Avg Close Time (days)
- Bucket A %

**Pipeline Summary (4 cards):**
- Pipeline Value ($)
- Active Deals
- Overdue Follow-ups
- BAMFAM Violations

**Dashboard Metrics Computation** (useMemo):
- Response rate = past_cold_dm / total_dms
- Meetings booked = in 'booked' or later stages
- Close rate = won / (won + lost)
- Temperature calculations

**BAMFAM Check Logic:**
```typescript
const isBAMFAM = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity) => {
  const postDiscovery = ['discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract'];
  if (!postDiscovery.includes(opp.stage)) return false;
  if (!opp.next_meeting_at) return true;
  return new Date(opp.next_meeting_at) < new Date();
};
```
(BAMFAM = "Big Account, Meeting-Focused, Advancing Management" - flags deals past discovery with no upcoming meeting)

---

### 6. EXISTING "NEXT ACTION" / TASK-LIKE LOGIC

**Activity System** (not traditional tasks, but similar):
- Each opportunity has an `activities` timeline
- Activities have: `type`, `title`, `description`, `outcome`, `next_step`, `next_step_date`
- Next steps can have associated dates
- Timeline display shows next steps with arrow icon and date

**Bump System** (action tracking):
- `bump_number`: 0-4 tracking how many times cold DM has been bumped
- `last_bump_date`: ISO timestamp
- Visual progress indicator (4 dots)
- Records each bump action as an activity

**BAMFAM Warning** (next meeting action):
- Highlights opportunities past discovery stage with no scheduled next meeting
- Visual red alert in kanban and table views
- Shown in slide-over

**Overdue Follow-ups Metric:**
- Counts active pipeline opps where `next_meeting_at < now`

---

I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Here is the complete implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: "Actions" Tab for Sales Pipeline

### Overview

Add a 4th "Actions" tab as the **first** tab in the sales pipeline page. This tab computes the next action for every opportunity across all stages, filters out items that are not currently actionable, sorts by priority, and presents one-click action buttons. Everything lives inside the existing `page.tsx` file, following the existing pattern of `renderXxxTab()` functions and `useMemo` for computed data.

---

### Step 1: Add the `getNextAction()` function (~line 677, after `isBAMFAM`)

Define a pure helper function that takes a `SalesPipelineOpportunity` and returns an action descriptor object. Place it in the helpers section around line 677.

**Type definition** (add above the component or inline):

```typescript
type ActionPriority = 'urgent' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' | 'wait';

interface NextAction {
  label: string;           // e.g. "Bump #2", "Book Call"
  priority: ActionPriority;
  actionType: 'bump' | 'stage_change' | 'open_detail' | 'none';
  targetStage?: SalesPipelineStage;  // for stage_change actions
  isActionable: boolean;   // false = "Wait Xd" type items
  waitDays?: number;       // how many days until actionable
  sortScore: number;       // lower = higher priority (for sorting)
}
```

**Logic** (inside the component, as a `useCallback` or plain function):

```typescript
const getNextAction = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity): NextAction => {
  const now = new Date();
  const daysSinceContact = opp.last_contacted_at
    ? Math.floor((now.getTime() - new Date(opp.last_contacted_at).getTime()) / 86400000)
    : 999;
  const daysSinceBump = opp.last_bump_date
    ? Math.floor((now.getTime() - new Date(opp.last_bump_date).getTime()) / 86400000)
    : 999;
  const daysSinceCreated = Math.floor((now.getTime() - new Date(opp.created_at).getTime()) / 86400000);
  const bamfam = isBAMFAM(opp);
  const stage = opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage;

  // BAMFAM always urgent regardless of stage
  if (bamfam) {
    return { label: 'Book Next Meeting!', priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 0 };
  }

  switch (stage) {
    case 'cold_dm': {
      if (opp.bump_number >= 4) {
        return { label: 'Review -> Orbit', priority: 'medium', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'orbit', isActionable: true, sortScore: 30 };
      }
      if (opp.bump_number === 0) {
        return { label: 'Send First DM', priority: 'high', actionType: 'bump', isActionable: true, sortScore: 10 };
      }
      // Has been bumped, check staleness
      if (daysSinceBump >= 3 || !opp.last_bump_date) {
        return { label: `Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}`, priority: 'high', actionType: 'bump', isActionable: true, sortScore: 10 + (3 - Math.min(daysSinceBump, 3)) };
      }
      // Recent bump, not actionable yet
      const waitDays = 3 - daysSinceBump;
      return { label: `Wait ${waitDays}d`, priority: 'wait', actionType: 'none', isActionable: false, waitDays, sortScore: 100 };
    }
    case 'warm': {
      if (opp.warm_sub_state === 'interested') {
        return { label: 'Get TG Handle', priority: 'high', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 5 };
      }
      return { label: 'Follow Up', priority: 'high', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 15 };
    }
    case 'tg_intro':
      return { label: 'Book Call', priority: 'high', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'booked', isActionable: true, sortScore: 8 };
    case 'booked': {
      if (!opp.next_meeting_at) {
        return { label: 'Schedule Meeting', priority: 'high', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 7 };
      }
      return { label: 'Prep for Meeting', priority: 'low', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 50 };
    }
    case 'discovery_done': {
      if (!opp.proposal_sent_at) {
        return { label: 'Send Proposal', priority: 'high', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'proposal_sent', isActionable: true, sortScore: 6 };
      }
      return { label: 'Awaiting Proposal Review', priority: 'low', actionType: 'none', isActionable: false, sortScore: 60 };
    }
    case 'proposal_sent':
      return { label: 'Follow Up Proposal', priority: 'high', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 12 };
    case 'proposal_call':
      return { label: 'Close Deal', priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'v2_contract', isActionable: true, sortScore: 2 };
    case 'v2_contract':
      return { label: 'Get Signed', priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'v2_closed_won', isActionable: true, sortScore: 1 };
    case 'orbit': {
      if (daysSinceCreated > 90) {
        return { label: 'Resurrect Check', priority: 'low', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 70 };
      }
      return { label: 'In Orbit', priority: 'wait', actionType: 'none', isActionable: false, sortScore: 200 };
    }
    default:
      return { label: 'No Action', priority: 'wait', actionType: 'none', isActionable: false, sortScore: 999 };
  }
};
```

Key design decisions:
- `sortScore` is a single numeric value for deterministic sort order (lower = more urgent).
- BAMFAM always overrides any stage-specific logic (sortScore 0).
- `cold_dm` items with recent bumps get `isActionable: false` so they are filtered from the default view.
- `v2_closed_won`, `v2_closed_lost`, `nurture` stages return "No Action" and will never appear.

---

### Step 2: Add the `actionItems` computed memo (~line 288, after `dashboardMetrics`)

```typescript
const [actionFilter, setActionFilter] = useState<'all' | 'mine' | 'urgent'>('mine');

const actionItems = useMemo(() => {
  // Combine pipeline opportunities + outreach opportunities
  // For cold_dm: use outreachOpps if available (paginated), otherwise fall back to opportunities
  const allOpps = [
    ...opportunities.filter(o => o.stage !== 'cold_dm'),
    ...outreachOpps,
  ];

  const items = allOpps.map(opp => ({
    opp,
    action: getNextAction(opp),
  }));

  // Filter based on actionFilter
  let filtered = items;
  if (actionFilter === 'mine') {
    filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
      item.opp.owner_id === user?.id || !item.opp.owner_id
    );
  }
  if (actionFilter === 'urgent') {
    filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
      item.action.priority === 'urgent' || item.action.priority === 'high'
    );
  }

  // By default, filter out non-actionable (wait items)
  filtered = filtered.filter(item => item.action.isActionable);

  // Exclude closed won / closed lost / nurture
  filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
    !['v2_closed_won', 'v2_closed_lost', 'nurture'].includes(item.opp.stage)
  );

  // Sort by sortScore ascending (most urgent first), then by temperature descending
  filtered.sort((a, b) => {
    if (a.action.sortScore !== b.action.sortScore) return a.action.sortScore - b.action.sortScore;
    return (b.opp.temperature_score || 0) - (a.opp.temperature_score || 0);
  });

  return filtered;
}, [opportunities, outreachOpps, actionFilter, user?.id]);

const actionCount = actionItems.length;
```

**Important consideration**: The outreach tab uses paginated cold DMs (`outreachOpps`), not the full set. For the Actions tab, we have two choices:
- (A) Use what is loaded (outreachOpps from the paginated fetch) -- simpler but incomplete.
- (B) Create a separate fetch for all actionable cold DMs.

**Recommendation**: Use option (A) initially. The `opportunities` state from `fetchData()` already includes ALL non-cold_dm pipeline items. For cold DMs, `outreachOpps` will be populated when the user visits the Outreach tab. Add a one-time fetch when the Actions tab is first activated to ensure outreach data is loaded. This avoids loading thousands of cold DMs upfront.

Actually, re-reading the code: `SalesPipelineService.getAll()` returns ALL opportunities including cold_dm. The `opportunities` state on line 135 contains everything. Only the Outreach tab uses separate paginated fetching for display. So for the Actions tab, we should use `opportunities` directly (which has all cold DMs already loaded).

Revised approach:

```typescript
const actionItems = useMemo(() => {
  const items = opportunities.map(opp => ({
    opp,
    action: getNextAction(opp),
  }));

  let filtered = items;
  if (actionFilter === 'mine') {
    filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
      item.opp.owner_id === user?.id || !item.opp.owner_id
    );
  }
  if (actionFilter === 'urgent') {
    filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
      item.action.priority === 'urgent' || item.action.priority === 'high'
    );
  }

  // Filter out non-actionable and terminal stages
  filtered = filtered.filter(item => item.action.isActionable);
  filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
    !['v2_closed_won', 'v2_closed_lost', 'nurture'].includes(item.opp.stage)
  );

  // Apply search
  if (searchTerm) {
    filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
      item.opp.name.toLowerCase().includes(searchTerm.toLowerCase())
    );
  }

  filtered.sort((a, b) => {
    if (a.action.sortScore !== b.action.sortScore) return a.action.sortScore - b.action.sortScore;
    return (b.opp.temperature_score || 0) - (a.opp.temperature_score || 0);
  });

  return filtered;
}, [opportunities, actionFilter, user?.id, searchTerm]);
```

### Step 3: Add the `handleActionExecute()` handler (~line 568, after `handleRecalculateAll`)

This is the one-click action handler dispatched from the action buttons:

```typescript
const [executingAction, setExecutingAction] = useState<string | null>(null);

const handleActionExecute = async (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity, action: NextAction) => {
  if (executingAction) return;
  setExecutingAction(opp.id);
  try {
    switch (action.actionType) {
      case 'bump':
        await handleRecordBump(opp.id);
        break;
      case 'stage_change':
        if (action.targetStage) {
          await handleStageChange(opp.id, action.targetStage, opp.stage);
        }
        break;
      case 'open_detail':
        openSlideOver(opp);
        break;
    }
  } finally {
    setExecutingAction(null);
  }
};
```

This reuses the existing `handleRecordBump`, `handleStageChange`, and `openSlideOver` functions directly. No new service methods needed.

### Step 5: Add the `renderActionsTab()` function (~line 1230, before `renderOutreachTab`)

This follows the same pattern as the other render functions. Structure:

```typescript
const renderActionsTab = () => (
  <div className="pb-8">
    {/* Filter sub-tabs */}
    <div className="flex items-center gap-1 mb-4">
      <button onClick={() => setActionFilter('all')}
        className={`px-3 py-1.5 text-xs font-medium rounded-md transition-colors ${
          actionFilter === 'all' ? 'bg-sky-100 text-sky-700 border border-sky-200' : 'text-gray-600 hover:bg-gray-100 border border-transparent'
        }`}>
        All Actions <span className="ml-1.5 text-[10px] font-semibold opacity-70">{/* count for all */}</span>
      </button>
      <button onClick={() => setActionFilter('mine')}
        className={/* same pattern */}>
        My Actions
      </button>
      <button onClick={() => setActionFilter('urgent')}
        className={/* same pattern */}>
        Urgent Only
      </button>
    </div>

    {/* Actions table */}
    <div className="border rounded-lg overflow-hidden">
      <Table>
        <TableHeader>
          <TableRow className="bg-gray-50/80">
            <TableHead className="w-[200px]">Name</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[100px]">Stage</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[60px]">Bucket</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[180px]">Next Action</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[80px]">Days Silent</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[60px]">Temp</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[50px]">Owner</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[120px] text-right">Execute</TableHead>
          </TableRow>
        </TableHeader>
        <TableBody>
          {actionItems.length === 0 ? (
            <TableRow>
              <TableCell colSpan={8} className="text-center py-12 text-gray-400">
                No actions needed right now. Nice work!
              </TableCell>
            </TableRow>
          ) : (
            actionItems.map(({ opp, action }) => {
              const colors = STAGE_COLORS[opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage] || STAGE_COLORS.cold_dm;
              const daysSinceContact = opp.last_contacted_at
                ? Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(opp.last_contacted_at).getTime()) / 86400000)
                : null;

              return (
                <TableRow
                  key={opp.id}
                  className={`group hover:bg-gray-50 cursor-pointer ${
                    action.priority === 'urgent' ? 'bg-red-50/30' : ''
                  }`}
                  onClick={() => openSlideOver(opp)}
                >
                  <TableCell className="font-medium">{opp.name}</TableCell>
                  <TableCell>
                    <Badge className={`${colors.bg} ${colors.text} border ${colors.border}`}>
                      {STAGE_LABELS[opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage]}
                    </Badge>
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell>
                    {opp.bucket && (
                      <Badge className={`${BUCKET_COLORS[opp.bucket].bg} ${BUCKET_COLORS[opp.bucket].text}`}>
                        {opp.bucket}
                      </Badge>
                    )}
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell>
                    <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
                      {action.priority === 'urgent' && <AlertTriangle className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-red-500" />}
                      {action.priority === 'high' && <Zap className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-amber-500" />}
                      <span className={`text-sm font-medium ${
                        action.priority === 'urgent' ? 'text-red-600' :
                        action.priority === 'high' ? 'text-amber-600' : 'text-gray-600'
                      }`}>
                        {action.label}
                      </span>
                    </div>
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell className="text-center">
                    {daysSinceContact !== null ? (
                      <span className={daysSinceContact > 5 ? 'text-red-500 font-medium' : 'text-gray-500'}>
                        {daysSinceContact}d
                      </span>
                    ) : '—'}
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell>
                    <span className={`text-sm font-medium ${
                      opp.temperature_score >= 70 ? 'text-red-600' :
                      opp.temperature_score >= 40 ? 'text-amber-600' : 'text-blue-600'
                    }`}>
                      {opp.temperature_score}
                    </span>
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell className="text-xs text-gray-500">
                    {getUserName(opp.owner_id)}
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell className="text-right" onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}>
                    <Button
                      size="sm"
                      variant={action.priority === 'urgent' ? 'destructive' : action.priority === 'high' ? 'default' : 'outline'}
                      className="h-7 text-xs"
                      disabled={executingAction === opp.id}
                      onClick={() => handleActionExecute(opp, action)}
                    >
                      {executingAction === opp.id ? (
                        <Loader2 className="h-3 w-3 animate-spin" />
                      ) : (
                        <>
                          {action.actionType === 'bump' && <Zap className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" />}
                          {action.actionType === 'stage_change' && <ArrowRight className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" />}
                          {action.actionType === 'open_detail' && <Edit className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" />}
                          {action.label}
                        </>
                      )}
                    </Button>
                  </TableCell>
                </TableRow>
              );
            })
          )}
        </TableBody>
      </Table>
    </div>
  </div>
);
```

### Edge cases and considerations

1. **Orbit prompt dialog**: When clicking "Review -> Orbit" one-click button, `handleStageChange` already intercepts orbit moves and opens the orbit reason prompt dialog. This works automatically.

2. **Data freshness after action**: `handleRecordBump` and `handleStageChange` already call `fetchData()`, which updates `opportunities`, which triggers `actionItems` recompute. The item will either disappear (cold_dm bump makes it non-actionable for 3d) or change action.

3. **Outreach cold DMs**: The `opportunities` state from `getAll()` includes ALL cold_dm records. This is fine for the actions tab since we filter to only actionable ones (no recent bumps). If the total cold_dm count is very large (thousands), the useMemo will still be fast since it is just array filtering.

4. **Temperature score display**: Reuses the same color logic already used in the pipeline table (hot=red, warm=amber, cold=blue).

5. **Empty state**: When no actions are needed, show a congratulatory message rather than a blank table.

---

Design an implementation plan for adding a task/action-oriented section to the sales pipeline page at /Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx.

## Context

The user has a sales pipeline CRM with 3 tabs: Outreach (cold DMs table), Pipeline (kanban/table of active deals), Orbit (parked deals). They want to add a task-oriented section inspired by a HoloHive CRM demo that has these key patterns:

1. **`getNextAction()` function** — computes what to do next per item based on state (stage, bump count, days since contact, meetings, etc.)
2. **"My Actions" view** — shows only items needing attention NOW
3. **Inline action buttons** — one-click to execute the next action
4. **Priority sorting** — hot leads first, stale deals flagged

## Current codebase context

**Existing data model** (SalesPipelineOpportunity):
- stage: cold_dm | warm | tg_intro | booked | discovery_done | proposal_sent | proposal_call | v2_contract | v2_closed_won | orbit | v2_closed_lost | nurture
- bucket: A | B | C | null
- bump_number: 0-N
- last_bump_date, last_contacted_at, created_at
- warm_sub_state: 'interested' | 'silent' | null
- next_meeting_at, next_meeting_type
- temperature_score: 0-100 (auto-calculated)
- dm_account: closer | sdr | other
- tg_handle, calendly_sent_via, calendly_sent_date, calendly_booked_date

**Existing actions already implemented:**
- `SalesPipelineService.recordBump(id)` — bump a cold DM
- `SalesPipelineService.update(id, updates)` — change stage, fields
- `SalesPipelineService.createActivity(data)` — log activity
- Bulk bump, bulk stage change, bulk delete
- BAMFAM detection (post-discovery with no upcoming meeting)

**Existing UI patterns:**
- Tabs component (TabsList/TabsTrigger/TabsContent)
- Table component for rows
- Slide-over panel (480px right side) for detail/edit/activity
- Dashboard section (collapsible Card)
- Badge, Button, Checkbox, DropdownMenu, Tooltip components
- Icons from lucide-react (already imported: Zap, Target, AlertTriangle, Clock, RotateCcw, etc.)
- DnD kit for drag/drop

**State management:**
- `opportunities` state has ALL pipeline opps
- `outreachOpps` has paginated cold DMs
- `fetchData()` and `fetchOutreach()` for refreshing
- `activeTab` controls which tab is shown
- `slideOverOpp` opens the detail panel

## Design requirements

The new "Actions" tab should:

1. Show a flat, priority-sorted list of opportunities that need action NOW
2. Each row shows: name, stage badge, next action label, days since contact, temperature, one-click action button
3. **getNextAction() logic** mapping:
   - cold_dm + bump_number=0 → "Send First DM"
   - cold_dm + last_bump stale (>=3 days or no bump date) → "Bump #N"  
   - cold_dm + last_bump recent (<3d) → "Wait Xd" (not actionable, filtered out)
   - cold_dm + bump_number>=4 → "Review → Orbit"
   - warm + interested → "Get TG Handle"
   - warm + silent → "Follow Up"
   - tg_intro → "Book Call"
   - booked + no next_meeting → "Schedule Meeting"
   - booked + next_meeting future → "Prep for Meeting" (lower priority)
   - discovery_done + no proposal_sent_at → "Send Proposal"
   - BAMFAM violation (post-discovery, no future meeting) → "Book Next Meeting!" (urgent)
   - proposal_sent → "Follow Up Proposal"
   - proposal_call → "Close Deal"
   - v2_contract → "Get Signed"
   - orbit + created >90 days ago → "Resurrect Check"
4. Filter buttons: "All Actions", "My Actions", "Urgent Only"
5. One-click action buttons that execute the action (bump, stage change, etc.)
6. Action count badge on the tab itself

This should be a NEW 4th tab added before the existing tabs, since it's the primary workflow view. Everything is in the single page.tsx file — no new files needed.

Please design a detailed implementation plan.

    91→    opacity: isDragging ? 0.5 : 1,
    92→  };
    93→  return (
    94→    <div ref={setNodeRef} style={style} {...attributes} {...listeners}>
    95→      {children}
    96→    </div>
    97→  );
    98→}
    99→
   100→function SortableTableRow({ id, children, className, onClick }: { id: string; children: React.ReactNode; className?: string; onClick?: () => void }) {
   101→  const { attributes, listeners, setNodeRef, transform, transition, isDragging } = useSortable({ id });
   102→  const style: React.CSSProperties = {
   103→    transform: CSS.Translate.toString(transform),
   104→    transition,
   105→    opacity: isDragging ? 0.5 : 1,
   106→    position: 'relative' as const,
   107→    zIndex: isDragging ? 1 : 0,
   108→  };
   109→  return (
   110→    <TableRow ref={setNodeRef} style={style} className={`${className || ''} ${isDragging ? 'bg-blue-50 shadow-lg' : ''}`} onClick={onClick}>
   111→      <TableCell className="w-10">
   112→        <div
   113→          {...attributes}
   114→          {...listeners}
   115→          className="cursor-grab active:cursor-grabbing p-1 hover:bg-gray-100 rounded"
   116→          onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}
   117→        >
   118→          <GripVertical className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
   119→        </div>
   120→      </TableCell>
   121→      {children}
   122→    </TableRow>
   123→  );
   124→}
   125→
   126→// ============================================
   127→// Main Page
   128→// ============================================
   129→

   132→
   133→  // Data state

   151→// ============================================

   155→export class SalesPipelineService {
   156→  // ----------------------------------------
   157→  // OPPORTUNITIES
   158→  // ----------------------------------------
   159→
   160→  static async getAll(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]> {
   161→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   162→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   163→      .select(`
   164→        *,
   165→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   166→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   167→      `)
   168→      .in('stage', ALL_V2_STAGES)
   169→      .order('position', { ascending: true })
   170→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   171→
   172→    if (error) {
   173→      console.error('Error fetching sales pipeline opportunities:', error);
   174→      throw error;
   175→    }
   176→    return (data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[];
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  static async create(data: CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   180→    // Dedup check: name + tg_handle
   181→    if (data.name && data.tg_handle) {
   182→      const dedupKey = `${data.name.toLowerCase().trim()}::${data.tg_handle.toLowerCase().trim()}`;
   183→      const { data: existing } = await supabase
   184→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   185→        .select('id')
   186→        .eq('dedup_key', dedupKey)
   187→        .maybeSingle();
   188→
   189→      if (existing) {
   190→        throw new Error('A deal with this name and TG handle already exists');
   191→      }
   192→
   193→      (data as any).dedup_key = dedupKey;
   194→    }
   195→
   196→    const insertData = {
   197→      ...data,
   198→      stage: data.stage || 'cold_dm',
   199→    };
   200→

   200→
   201→  // DnD sensors
   202→  const sensors = useSensors(
   203→    useSensor(PointerSensor, { activationConstraint: { distance: 8 } }),
   204→    useSensor(KeyboardSensor)
   205→  );
   206→
   207→  // ============================================
   208→  // Dashboard Metrics (computed from opportunities)
   209→  // ============================================
   210→
   211→  const dashboardMetrics = useMemo(() => {
   212→    const all = opportunities;
   213→    const pipelineActive = all.filter(o => PIPELINE_STAGES.includes(o.stage as SalesPipelineStage));
   214→    const closedWon = all.filter(o => o.stage === 'v2_closed_won');
   215→    const closedLost = all.filter(o => o.stage === 'v2_closed_lost');
   216→    const inOrbit = all.filter(o => o.stage === 'orbit');
   217→
   218→    // Stages past cold_dm = responded
   219→    const pastColdDm = all.filter(o => o.stage !== 'cold_dm');
   220→    const coldDmCount = outreachTotal > 0 ? outreachTotal : all.filter(o => o.stage === 'cold_dm').length;
   221→    const totalDmsSent = all.length; // every opp started as a DM
   222→    const responseRate = totalDmsSent > 0 ? (pastColdDm.length / totalDmsSent) * 100 : 0;
   223→
   224→    // Meetings booked = in booked or any stage after it
   225→    const bookedAndBeyond = ['booked', 'discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract', 'v2_closed_won'];
   226→    const meetingsBooked = all.filter(o => bookedAndBeyond.includes(o.stage)).length;
   227→
   228→    // Discovery calls = in discovery_done or later
   229→    const discoveryAndBeyond = ['discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract', 'v2_closed_won'];
   230→    const discoveryCalls = all.filter(o => discoveryAndBeyond.includes(o.stage)).length;
   231→
   232→    // Close rate = won / (won + lost)
   233→    const totalClosed = closedWon.length + closedLost.length;
   234→    const closeRate = totalClosed > 0 ? (closedWon.length / totalClosed) * 100 : 0;
   235→
   236→    // Avg deal size (closed won)
   237→    const wonValues = closedWon.map(o => o.deal_value || 0).filter(v => v > 0);
   238→    const avgDealSize = wonValues.length > 0 ? wonValues.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / wonValues.length : 0;
   239→
   240→    // Avg close time (days from created_at to closed_at for won deals)
   241→    const closeTimes = closedWon
   242→      .filter(o => o.created_at && o.closed_at)
   243→      .map(o => (new Date(o.closed_at!).getTime() - new Date(o.created_at).getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
   244→    const avgCloseTime = closeTimes.length > 0 ? closeTimes.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / closeTimes.length : 0;
   245→
   246→    // Qualified % = bucket A + B / total
   247→    const qualifiedCount = all.filter(o => o.bucket === 'A' || o.bucket === 'B').length;
   248→    const qualifiedPct = all.length > 0 ? (qualifiedCount / all.length) * 100 : 0;
   249→
   250→    // Bucket A %
   251→    const bucketAPct = all.length > 0 ? (all.filter(o => o.bucket === 'A').length / all.length) * 100 : 0;
   252→
   253→    // Pipeline value (active)
   254→    const pipelineValue = pipelineActive.reduce((sum, o) => sum + (o.deal_value || 0), 0);
   255→
   256→    // Overdue follow-ups
   257→    const now = new Date().toISOString();
   258→    const overdueFollowups = pipelineActive.filter(o =>
   259→      o.next_meeting_at && o.next_meeting_at < now
   260→    ).length;
   261→
   262→    // Proposals sent
   263→    const proposalsSent = all.filter(o =>
   264→      ['proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract', 'v2_closed_won'].includes(o.stage)
   265→    ).length;
   266→
   267→    return {
   268→      totalDmsSent,
   269→      responseRate,
   270→      meetingsBooked,
   271→      discoveryCalls,
   272→      closeRate,
   273→      avgDealSize,
   274→      avgCloseTime,
   275→      qualifiedPct,
   276→      bucketAPct,
   277→      pipelineValue,
   278→      activeDeals: pipelineActive.length,
   279→      overdueFollowups,
   280→      bamfamViolations: metrics.bamfamViolations,
   281→      closedWon: closedWon.length,
   282→      closedLost: closedLost.length,
   283→      inOrbit: inOrbit.length,
   284→      proposalsSent,
   285→      coldDmCount,
   286→    };
   287→  }, [opportunities, metrics.bamfamViolations, outreachTotal]);
   288→

   217→
   218→  static async update(
   219→    id: string,
   220→    updates: Partial<CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData> & {
   221→      warm_sub_state?: WarmSubState | null;
   222→      orbit_reason?: OrbitReason | null;
   223→      closed_lost_reason?: string | null;
   224→      next_meeting_at?: string | null;
   225→      next_meeting_type?: string | null;
   226→      bump_number?: number;
   227→      last_bump_date?: string | null;
   228→      bucket?: Bucket | null;
   229→      calendly_sent_via?: CalendlySentVia;
   230→      calendly_sent_date?: string | null;
   231→      calendly_booked_date?: string | null;
   232→      gc_opened?: string;
   233→    }
   234→  ): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   235→    // Get current state for stage history
   236→    const { data: current } = await supabase
   237→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   238→      .select('*')
   239→      .eq('id', id)
   240→      .single();
   241→
   242→    const updateData: any = { ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
   243→
   244→    // Auto-set timestamps on stage transitions
   245→    if (updates.stage && current) {
   246→      if (updates.stage === 'discovery_done' && !current.discovery_call_at) {
   247→        updateData.discovery_call_at = new Date().toISOString();
   248→      }
   249→      if (updates.stage === 'proposal_sent' && !current.proposal_sent_at) {
   250→        updateData.proposal_sent_at = new Date().toISOString();
   251→      }
   252→      if ((updates.stage === 'v2_closed_won' || updates.stage === 'v2_closed_lost') && !current.closed_at) {
   253→        updateData.closed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   254→      }
   255→    }
   256→
   257→    // Auto-set bucket_changed_at
   258→    if (updates.bucket && current && updates.bucket !== current.bucket) {
   259→      updateData.bucket_changed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    const { data: updated, error } = await supabase
   263→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   264→      .update(updateData)
   265→      .eq('id', id)
   266→      .select()
   267→      .single();
   268→
   269→    if (error) throw error;
   270→
   271→    // Record stage change
   272→    if (updates.stage && current && current.stage !== updates.stage) {
   273→      await CRMService.recordStageHistory('opportunity', id, current.stage, updates.stage);
   274→    }
   275→
   276→    // Recalculate temperature score after stage/bucket/key field changes
   277→    await this.recalcTemperature(id);
   278→
   279→    return updated as SalesPipelineOpportunity;
   280→  }
   281→
   282→  static async delete(id: string): Promise<void> {
   283→    const { error } = await supabase
   284→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   285→      .delete()
   286→      .eq('id', id);
   287→
   288→    if (error) throw error;
   289→  }
   290→

   292→  // BUMPS

   294→
   295→  static async recordBump(id: string): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   296→    const { data: current } = await supabase
   297→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   298→      .select('bump_number')
   299→      .eq('id', id)
   300→      .single();
   301→
   302→    const newBumpNumber = (current?.bump_number || 0) + 1;
   303→
   304→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   305→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   306→      .update({
   307→        bump_number: newBumpNumber,
   308→        last_bump_date: new Date().toISOString(),
   309→        last_contacted_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   310→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   311→      })
   312→      .eq('id', id)
   313→      .select()
   314→      .single();
   315→
   316→    if (error) throw error;
   317→
   318→    // Also create a bump activity
   319→    await this.createActivity({
   320→      opportunity_id: id,
   321→      type: 'bump',
   322→      title: `Bump #${newBumpNumber}`,
   323→    });
   324→
   325→    // Recalculate temperature score
   326→    await this.recalcTemperature(id);
   327→
   328→    return data as SalesPipelineOpportunity;
   329→  }
   330→
   331→  static async reduceBump(id: string): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   332→    const { data: current } = await supabase
   333→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   334→      .select('bump_number')
   335→      .eq('id', id)
   336→      .single();
   337→
   338→    const newBumpNumber = Math.max((current?.bump_number || 0) - 1, 0);
   339→
   340→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   341→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   342→      .update({
   343→        bump_number: newBumpNumber,
   344→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   345→      })
   346→      .eq('id', id)
   347→      .select()
   348→      .single();

I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Here is the complete implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: "Actions" Tab for Sales Pipeline

### Overview

Add a 4th "Actions" tab as the **first** tab in the sales pipeline page. This tab computes the next action for every opportunity across all stages, filters out items that are not currently actionable, sorts by priority, and presents one-click action buttons. Everything lives inside the existing `page.tsx` file, following the existing pattern of `renderXxxTab()` functions and `useMemo` for computed data.

---

### Step 1: Add the `getNextAction()` function (~line 677, after `isBAMFAM`)

Define a pure helper function that takes a `SalesPipelineOpportunity` and returns an action descriptor object. Place it in the helpers section around line 677.

**Type definition** (add above the component or inline):

```typescript
type ActionPriority = 'urgent' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' | 'wait';

interface NextAction {
  label: string;           // e.g. "Bump #2", "Book Call"
  priority: ActionPriority;
  actionType: 'bump' | 'stage_change' | 'open_detail' | 'none';
  targetStage?: SalesPipelineStage;  // for stage_change actions
  isActionable: boolean;   // false = "Wait Xd" type items
  waitDays?: number;       // how many days until actionable
  sortScore: number;       // lower = higher priority (for sorting)
}
```

**Logic** (inside the component, as a `useCallback` or plain function):

```typescript
const getNextAction = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity): NextAction => {
  const now = new Date();
  const daysSinceContact = opp.last_contacted_at
    ? Math.floor((now.getTime() - new Date(opp.last_contacted_at).getTime()) / 86400000)
    : 999;
  const daysSinceBump = opp.last_bump_date
    ? Math.floor((now.getTime() - new Date(opp.last_bump_date).getTime()) / 86400000)
    : 999;
  const daysSinceCreated = Math.floor((now.getTime() - new Date(opp.created_at).getTime()) / 86400000);
  const bamfam = isBAMFAM(opp);
  const stage = opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage;

  // BAMFAM always urgent regardless of stage
  if (bamfam) {
    return { label: 'Book Next Meeting!', priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 0 };
  }

  switch (stage) {
    case 'cold_dm': {
      if (opp.bump_number >= 4) {
        return { label: 'Review -> Orbit', priority: 'medium', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'orbit', isActionable: true, sortScore: 30 };
      }
      if (opp.bump_number === 0) {
        return { label: 'Send First DM', priority: 'high', actionType: 'bump', isActionable: true, sortScore: 10 };
      }
      // Has been bumped, check staleness
      if (daysSinceBump >= 3 || !opp.last_bump_date) {
        return { label: `Bump #${opp.bump_number + 1}`, priority: 'high', actionType: 'bump', isActionable: true, sortScore: 10 + (3 - Math.min(daysSinceBump, 3)) };
      }
      // Recent bump, not actionable yet
      const waitDays = 3 - daysSinceBump;
      return { label: `Wait ${waitDays}d`, priority: 'wait', actionType: 'none', isActionable: false, waitDays, sortScore: 100 };
    }
    case 'warm': {
      if (opp.warm_sub_state === 'interested') {
        return { label: 'Get TG Handle', priority: 'high', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 5 };
      }
      return { label: 'Follow Up', priority: 'high', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 15 };
    }
    case 'tg_intro':
      return { label: 'Book Call', priority: 'high', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'booked', isActionable: true, sortScore: 8 };
    case 'booked': {
      if (!opp.next_meeting_at) {
        return { label: 'Schedule Meeting', priority: 'high', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 7 };
      }
      return { label: 'Prep for Meeting', priority: 'low', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 50 };
    }
    case 'discovery_done': {
      if (!opp.proposal_sent_at) {
        return { label: 'Send Proposal', priority: 'high', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'proposal_sent', isActionable: true, sortScore: 6 };
      }
      return { label: 'Awaiting Proposal Review', priority: 'low', actionType: 'none', isActionable: false, sortScore: 60 };
    }
    case 'proposal_sent':
      return { label: 'Follow Up Proposal', priority: 'high', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 12 };
    case 'proposal_call':
      return { label: 'Close Deal', priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'v2_contract', isActionable: true, sortScore: 2 };
    case 'v2_contract':
      return { label: 'Get Signed', priority: 'urgent', actionType: 'stage_change', targetStage: 'v2_closed_won', isActionable: true, sortScore: 1 };
    case 'orbit': {
      if (daysSinceCreated > 90) {
        return { label: 'Resurrect Check', priority: 'low', actionType: 'open_detail', isActionable: true, sortScore: 70 };
      }
      return { label: 'In Orbit', priority: 'wait', actionType: 'none', isActionable: false, sortScore: 200 };
    }
    default:
      return { label: 'No Action', priority: 'wait', actionType: 'none', isActionable: false, sortScore: 999 };
  }
};
```

Key design decisions:
- `sortScore` is a single numeric value for deterministic sort order (lower = more urgent).
- BAMFAM always overrides any stage-specific logic (sortScore 0).
- `cold_dm` items with recent bumps get `isActionable: false` so they are filtered from the default view.
- `v2_closed_won`, `v2_closed_lost`, `nurture` stages return "No Action" and will never appear.

---

### Step 2: Add the `actionItems` computed memo (~line 288, after `dashboardMetrics`)

```typescript
const [actionFilter, setActionFilter] = useState<'all' | 'mine' | 'urgent'>('mine');

const actionItems = useMemo(() => {
  // Combine pipeline opportunities + outreach opportunities
  // For cold_dm: use outreachOpps if available (paginated), otherwise fall back to opportunities
  const allOpps = [
    ...opportunities.filter(o => o.stage !== 'cold_dm'),
    ...outreachOpps,
  ];

  const items = allOpps.map(opp => ({
    opp,
    action: getNextAction(opp),
  }));

  // Filter based on actionFilter
  let filtered = items;
  if (actionFilter === 'mine') {
    filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
      item.opp.owner_id === user?.id || !item.opp.owner_id
    );
  }
  if (actionFilter === 'urgent') {
    filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
      item.action.priority === 'urgent' || item.action.priority === 'high'
    );
  }

  // By default, filter out non-actionable (wait items)
  filtered = filtered.filter(item => item.action.isActionable);

  // Exclude closed won / closed lost / nurture
  filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
    !['v2_closed_won', 'v2_closed_lost', 'nurture'].includes(item.opp.stage)
  );

  // Sort by sortScore ascending (most urgent first), then by temperature descending
  filtered.sort((a, b) => {
    if (a.action.sortScore !== b.action.sortScore) return a.action.sortScore - b.action.sortScore;
    return (b.opp.temperature_score || 0) - (a.opp.temperature_score || 0);
  });

  return filtered;
}, [opportunities, outreachOpps, actionFilter, user?.id]);

const actionCount = actionItems.length;
```

**Important consideration**: The outreach tab uses paginated cold DMs (`outreachOpps`), not the full set. For the Actions tab, we have two choices:
- (A) Use what is loaded (outreachOpps from the paginated fetch) -- simpler but incomplete.
- (B) Create a separate fetch for all actionable cold DMs.

**Recommendation**: Use option (A) initially. The `opportunities` state from `fetchData()` already includes ALL non-cold_dm pipeline items. For cold DMs, `outreachOpps` will be populated when the user visits the Outreach tab. Add a one-time fetch when the Actions tab is first activated to ensure outreach data is loaded. This avoids loading thousands of cold DMs upfront.

Actually, re-reading the code: `SalesPipelineService.getAll()` returns ALL opportunities including cold_dm. The `opportunities` state on line 135 contains everything. Only the Outreach tab uses separate paginated fetching for display. So for the Actions tab, we should use `opportunities` directly (which has all cold DMs already loaded).

Revised approach:

```typescript
const actionItems = useMemo(() => {
  const items = opportunities.map(opp => ({
    opp,
    action: getNextAction(opp),
  }));

  let filtered = items;
  if (actionFilter === 'mine') {
    filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
      item.opp.owner_id === user?.id || !item.opp.owner_id
    );
  }
  if (actionFilter === 'urgent') {
    filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
      item.action.priority === 'urgent' || item.action.priority === 'high'
    );
  }

  // Filter out non-actionable and terminal stages
  filtered = filtered.filter(item => item.action.isActionable);
  filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
    !['v2_closed_won', 'v2_closed_lost', 'nurture'].includes(item.opp.stage)
  );

  // Apply search
  if (searchTerm) {
    filtered = filtered.filter(item =>
      item.opp.name.toLowerCase().includes(searchTerm.toLowerCase())
    );
  }

  filtered.sort((a, b) => {
    if (a.action.sortScore !== b.action.sortScore) return a.action.sortScore - b.action.sortScore;
    return (b.opp.temperature_score || 0) - (a.opp.temperature_score || 0);
  });

  return filtered;
}, [opportunities, actionFilter, user?.id, searchTerm]);
```

### Step 3: Add the `handleActionExecute()` handler (~line 568, after `handleRecalculateAll`)

This is the one-click action handler dispatched from the action buttons:

```typescript
const [executingAction, setExecutingAction] = useState<string | null>(null);

const handleActionExecute = async (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity, action: NextAction) => {
  if (executingAction) return;
  setExecutingAction(opp.id);
  try {
    switch (action.actionType) {
      case 'bump':
        await handleRecordBump(opp.id);
        break;
      case 'stage_change':
        if (action.targetStage) {
          await handleStageChange(opp.id, action.targetStage, opp.stage);
        }
        break;
      case 'open_detail':
        openSlideOver(opp);
        break;
    }
  } finally {
    setExecutingAction(null);
  }
};
```

This reuses the existing `handleRecordBump`, `handleStageChange`, and `openSlideOver` functions directly. No new service methods needed.

### Step 5: Add the `renderActionsTab()` function (~line 1230, before `renderOutreachTab`)

This follows the same pattern as the other render functions. Structure:

```typescript
const renderActionsTab = () => (
  <div className="pb-8">
    {/* Filter sub-tabs */}
    <div className="flex items-center gap-1 mb-4">
      <button onClick={() => setActionFilter('all')}
        className={`px-3 py-1.5 text-xs font-medium rounded-md transition-colors ${
          actionFilter === 'all' ? 'bg-sky-100 text-sky-700 border border-sky-200' : 'text-gray-600 hover:bg-gray-100 border border-transparent'
        }`}>
        All Actions <span className="ml-1.5 text-[10px] font-semibold opacity-70">{/* count for all */}</span>
      </button>
      <button onClick={() => setActionFilter('mine')}
        className={/* same pattern */}>
        My Actions
      </button>
      <button onClick={() => setActionFilter('urgent')}
        className={/* same pattern */}>
        Urgent Only
      </button>
    </div>

    {/* Actions table */}
    <div className="border rounded-lg overflow-hidden">
      <Table>
        <TableHeader>
          <TableRow className="bg-gray-50/80">
            <TableHead className="w-[200px]">Name</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[100px]">Stage</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[60px]">Bucket</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[180px]">Next Action</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[80px]">Days Silent</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[60px]">Temp</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[50px]">Owner</TableHead>
            <TableHead className="w-[120px] text-right">Execute</TableHead>
          </TableRow>
        </TableHeader>
        <TableBody>
          {actionItems.length === 0 ? (
            <TableRow>
              <TableCell colSpan={8} className="text-center py-12 text-gray-400">
                No actions needed right now. Nice work!
              </TableCell>
            </TableRow>
          ) : (
            actionItems.map(({ opp, action }) => {
              const colors = STAGE_COLORS[opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage] || STAGE_COLORS.cold_dm;
              const daysSinceContact = opp.last_contacted_at
                ? Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(opp.last_contacted_at).getTime()) / 86400000)
                : null;

              return (
                <TableRow
                  key={opp.id}
                  className={`group hover:bg-gray-50 cursor-pointer ${
                    action.priority === 'urgent' ? 'bg-red-50/30' : ''
                  }`}
                  onClick={() => openSlideOver(opp)}
                >
                  <TableCell className="font-medium">{opp.name}</TableCell>
                  <TableCell>
                    <Badge className={`${colors.bg} ${colors.text} border ${colors.border}`}>
                      {STAGE_LABELS[opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage]}
                    </Badge>
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell>
                    {opp.bucket && (
                      <Badge className={`${BUCKET_COLORS[opp.bucket].bg} ${BUCKET_COLORS[opp.bucket].text}`}>
                        {opp.bucket}
                      </Badge>
                    )}
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell>
                    <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
                      {action.priority === 'urgent' && <AlertTriangle className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-red-500" />}
                      {action.priority === 'high' && <Zap className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-amber-500" />}
                      <span className={`text-sm font-medium ${
                        action.priority === 'urgent' ? 'text-red-600' :
                        action.priority === 'high' ? 'text-amber-600' : 'text-gray-600'
                      }`}>
                        {action.label}
                      </span>
                    </div>
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell className="text-center">
                    {daysSinceContact !== null ? (
                      <span className={daysSinceContact > 5 ? 'text-red-500 font-medium' : 'text-gray-500'}>
                        {daysSinceContact}d
                      </span>
                    ) : '—'}
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell>
                    <span className={`text-sm font-medium ${
                      opp.temperature_score >= 70 ? 'text-red-600' :
                      opp.temperature_score >= 40 ? 'text-amber-600' : 'text-blue-600'
                    }`}>
                      {opp.temperature_score}
                    </span>
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell className="text-xs text-gray-500">
                    {getUserName(opp.owner_id)}
                  </TableCell>
                  <TableCell className="text-right" onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}>
                    <Button
                      size="sm"
                      variant={action.priority === 'urgent' ? 'destructive' : action.priority === 'high' ? 'default' : 'outline'}
                      className="h-7 text-xs"
                      disabled={executingAction === opp.id}
                      onClick={() => handleActionExecute(opp, action)}
                    >
                      {executingAction === opp.id ? (
                        <Loader2 className="h-3 w-3 animate-spin" />
                      ) : (
                        <>
                          {action.actionType === 'bump' && <Zap className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" />}
                          {action.actionType === 'stage_change' && <ArrowRight className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" />}
                          {action.actionType === 'open_detail' && <Edit className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" />}
                          {action.label}
                        </>
                      )}
                    </Button>
                  </TableCell>
                </TableRow>
              );
            })
          )}
        </TableBody>
      </Table>
    </div>
  </div>
);
```

---

### Step 6: Update the Tabs UI (~line 2761)

Insert the Actions tab trigger as the **first** tab, before Outreach:

```tsx
<TabsTrigger value="actions" className="flex items-center gap-2">
  <Zap className="h-4 w-4" />
  Actions
  {actionCount > 0 && (
    <Badge variant="secondary" className="ml-1 bg-amber-100 text-amber-700">{actionCount}</Badge>
  )}
</TabsTrigger>
```

Add the TabsContent:

```tsx
<TabsContent value="actions" className="mt-0">
  {renderActionsTab()}
</TabsContent>
```

---

### Step 7: New state variables summary

All new state to add (around lines 142-197):

```typescript
// Actions tab
const [actionFilter, setActionFilter] = useState<'all' | 'mine' | 'urgent'>('mine');
const [executingAction, setExecutingAction] = useState<string | null>(null);
```

That is only 2 new state variables. The `actionItems` and `actionCount` are derived via `useMemo`.

---

### Step 8: Import additions

No new imports needed. All icons used (`Zap`, `AlertTriangle`, `ArrowRight`, `Edit`, `Loader2`, `Clock`) are already imported on lines 22-26. All UI components (`Table`, `Badge`, `Button`, `Tooltip`) are already imported.

---

### Sequencing of changes within page.tsx

1. **Line 142**: Change `activeTab` type and default value to `'actions'`
2. **Lines ~175-177**: Add `actionFilter` and `executingAction` state
3. **Lines ~288**: Add `actionItems` useMemo (after `dashboardMetrics`)
4. **Lines ~568**: Add `handleActionExecute` handler
5. **Lines ~677**: Add `getNextAction` function (after `isBAMFAM`)
6. **Lines ~1230**: Add `renderActionsTab()` function (before `renderOutreachTab`)
7. **Lines ~2761**: Add Actions TabsTrigger and TabsContent, update `onValueChange` type

---

### Edge cases and considerations

1. **Orbit prompt dialog**: When clicking "Review -> Orbit" one-click button, `handleStageChange` already intercepts orbit moves and opens the orbit reason prompt dialog. This works automatically.

2. **Data freshness after action**: `handleRecordBump` and `handleStageChange` already call `fetchData()`, which updates `opportunities`, which triggers `actionItems` recompute. The item will either disappear (cold_dm bump makes it non-actionable for 3d) or change action.

3. **Outreach cold DMs**: The `opportunities` state from `getAll()` includes ALL cold_dm records. This is fine for the actions tab since we filter to only actionable ones (no recent bumps). If the total cold_dm count is very large (thousands), the useMemo will still be fast since it is just array filtering.

4. **Temperature score display**: Reuses the same color logic already used in the pipeline table (hot=red, warm=amber, cold=blue).

5. **Empty state**: When no actions are needed, show a congratulatory message rather than a blank table.

---

- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` - The single file where ALL changes go: new state, getNextAction logic, actionItems memo, renderActionsTab function, tab UI additions
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/salesPipelineService.ts` - Reference for SalesPipelineOpportunity interface, stage constants, and existing service methods (no changes needed)
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/ui/badge.tsx` - Already-imported Badge component used for action count on tab and stage/bucket badges in action rows (no changes needed, pattern reference)

```text
     1→# Temperature Score Auto-Calculation
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Temperature scores are currently manual (UI slider, default 50). At 1000+ cold DMs, nobody is updating these by hand. We need an algorithm that auto-calculates `temperature_score` (0-100) based on engagement signals already in the DB, so scores stay fresh and reflect real lead health.
     6→
     7→## Algorithm
     8→
     9→**Score = base + recency + engagement + meeting + stage_bonus + penalties (clamped 0-100)**
    10→
    11→| Component | Logic | Points |
    12→|---|---|---|
    13→| **Base (bucket)** | A=40, B=25, C=10, null=20 | 10-40 |
    14→| **Recency** | `30 - days_since_last_contact` (floor 0) | 0-30 |
    15→| **Engagement** | `min(20, activity_count * 4)` | 0-20 |
    16→| **Meeting booked** | `next_meeting_at` is in the future | +15 |
    17→| **Stage bonus** | warm=+5, booked/tg_intro=+10, discovery+=+15 | 0-15 |
    18→| **Warm interested** | `warm_sub_state = 'interested'` | +10 |
    19→| **Stale penalty** | cold_dm + created >30 days ago | -15 |
    20→| **Bump exhaustion** | cold_dm + bump_number >= 3 | -10 |
    21→| **Silent warm** | `warm_sub_state = 'silent'` | -5 |
    22→| **Orbit/lost** | stage is orbit → 5, closed_lost → 0 | Override |
    23→
    24→## Approach — Postgres function + service-layer triggers
    25→
    26→### Step 1: Postgres function via Supabase migration
    27→
    28→Create `calculate_temperature_score(opp_id uuid)` as a plpgsql function:
    29→- Reads opp fields: stage, bucket, bump_number, last_contacted_at, next_meeting_at, warm_sub_state, created_at
    30→- Counts rows in `crm_activities` for that opp_id
    31→- Applies the weighted formula
    32→- Updates `temperature_score` on the `crm_opportunities` row
    33→- Returns the new score
    34→
    35→Also create `recalculate_all_temperature_scores()` that loops all active opps (stages in the pipeline + orbit).
    36→
    37→Applied via `mcp__supabase__apply_migration`.
    38→
    39→### Step 2: Service-layer integration in `lib/salesPipelineService.ts`
    40→
    41→Add two methods:
    42→- `static async recalcTemperature(id: string)` — calls `supabase.rpc('calculate_temperature_score', { opp_id: id })`
    43→- `static async recalcAllTemperatures()` — calls `supabase.rpc('recalculate_all_temperature_scores')`
    44→
    45→Call `recalcTemperature()` at the end of:
    46→- `recordBump()` — bump changes engagement signal
    47→- `update()` — when stage or bucket changes
    48→- `createActivity()` — new activity increases engagement
    49→
    50→### Step 3: UI changes in `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`
    51→
    52→- Add a "Recalculate Scores" button (small refresh icon) near the Sales Dashboard header for manual batch recalc
    53→- Remove the temperature slider from the "New Opportunity" create form (Details section) since it's now auto-calculated
    54→- Keep the slider in the edit form for manual override if needed
    55→
    56→## Files
    57→
    58→| File | Change |
    59→|---|---|
    60→| New migration via `apply_migration` | Postgres functions `calculate_temperature_score` + `recalculate_all_temperature_scores` |
    61→| `lib/salesPipelineService.ts` | Add `recalcTemperature()`, `recalcAllTemperatures()`, call after key mutations |
    62→| `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` | Add recalc button, remove temp slider from create form |
    63→
    64→## Verification
    65→
    66→1. Migration applies successfully
    67→2. Create a new opp → temperature auto-calculated based on signals
    68→3. Record a bump → temperature updates accordingly
    69→4. Change stage to warm → temperature increases
    70→5. Stale cold_dm (>30 days) → lower temperature
    71→6. "Recalculate Scores" button updates all opps
    72→7. `npm run build` passes
    73→

```md
# Action-Oriented Tasks Tab for Sales Pipeline

## Context

The sales pipeline currently has 3 tabs (Outreach, Pipeline, Orbit) that show data by stage. Inspired by the HoloHive CRM demo, we want to add a **task-oriented "Actions" tab** as the primary workflow view. Instead of "here are your leads by stage", it answers "what do I need to do right now?" with one-click execution.

## What Changes

**Single file**: `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` (no new files, no service changes)

### 1. `getNextAction()` helper function

Pure function that takes an opportunity and returns what to do next:

| Stage | Condition | Action Label | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| Any post-discovery | BAMFAM violation (no future meeting) | "Book Next Meeting!" | urgent |
| `v2_contract` | — | "Get Signed" | urgent |
| `proposal_call` | — | "Close Deal" | urgent |
| `cold_dm` | bump_number = 0 | "Send First DM" | high |
| `cold_dm` | last_bump >= 3 days ago | "Bump #N" | high |
| `cold_dm` | last_bump < 3 days | "Wait Xd" | wait (filtered out) |
| `cold_dm` | bump_number >= 4 | "Review → Orbit" | medium |
| `warm` | interested | "Get TG Handle" | high |
| `warm` | silent | "Follow Up" | high |
| `tg_intro` | — | "Book Call" | high |
| `booked` | no next_meeting | "Schedule Meeting" | high |
| `booked` | meeting scheduled | "Prep for Meeting" | low |
| `discovery_done` | no proposal_sent_at | "Send Proposal" | high |
| `proposal_sent` | — | "Follow Up Proposal" | high |
| `orbit` | created > 90 days ago | "Resurrect Check" | low |

Returns: `{ label, priority, actionType: 'bump' | 'stage_change' | 'open_detail' | 'none', targetStage?, isActionable, sortScore }`

### 2. `actionItems` useMemo

Derives from existing `opportunities` state (already has all opps loaded):
- Maps each opp through `getNextAction()`
- Filters by `actionFilter` state: "all" / "mine" / "urgent"
- Filters out non-actionable items (wait state) and terminal stages
- Sorts by `sortScore` ascending (most urgent first), then by temperature descending

### 3. `handleActionExecute()` handler

One-click dispatcher that reuses existing handlers:
- `actionType: 'bump'` → calls existing `handleRecordBump()`
- `actionType: 'stage_change'` → calls existing `handleStageChange()` (orbit/closed_lost prompts still work)
- `actionType: 'open_detail'` → opens existing slide-over panel

### 4. New state (2 variables only)

\```typescript
const [actionFilter, setActionFilter] = useState<'all' | 'mine' | 'urgent'>('mine');
const [executingAction, setExecutingAction] = useState<string | null>(null);
\```

### 5. Tab changes

- Change `activeTab` type to include `'actions'` and default to `'actions'`
- Add "Actions" as the **first** tab with a Zap icon and action count badge
- Add `renderActionsTab()` function

### 6. `renderActionsTab()` UI

- **Filter sub-tabs**: All Actions / My Actions / Urgent Only (pill buttons, same style as outreach owner sub-tabs)
- **Table** with columns: Name, Stage, Bucket, Next Action (with priority icon), Days Silent, Temp, Owner, Execute button
- Urgent rows get subtle red background tint
- Execute button style varies by priority (destructive for urgent, default for high, outline for low)
- Row click opens slide-over detail
- Empty state: "No actions needed right now" message
- No new imports needed — all icons and components already imported

## Verification

1. `npm run build` passes
2. Actions tab loads as default, shows prioritized list
3. "My Actions" filter shows only current user's opps
4. "Urgent Only" shows BAMFAM violations + close-stage items
5. Clicking "Bump #N" button executes bump and item moves to wait state (disappears)
6. Clicking "Review → Orbit" opens orbit reason dialog
7. Clicking "Get TG Handle" / "Schedule Meeting" opens slide-over
8. Stage change buttons advance the opp correctly

```
